topic.title: Introduction to VlppParser
topic.p: IMPORTANT: This library is deprecated and you are strongly recommended to use VlppParser2 instead. VlppParser2 offers better performance, more powerful features, almost compatible syntax of definition, and more importantly, it follows the same design principle to the APIs so it is very easy to migrate.
VlppParser provides GLR parser generator for C++, with generated XML and JSON parsers.
AST here means abstract syntax tree, which are strong-typed representation of text in a specific format.

topic.title: JSON parser

ul.li: JsonParse: Takes a JSON string and the return value from JsonLoadTable, returns the AST for the JSON string. You are recommended to cache the return value from JsonLoadTable to improve performance.
JsonPrint and JsonToString: Serialize JSON from AST back to string.

topic.p: IMPORTANT: The parser does not support surrogate pairs in HEX codes.

topic.title: XML parser

ul.li: XmlParse: Takes an XML string and the return value from XmlLoadTable, returns the AST for the XML string. You are recommended to cache the return value from XmlLoadTable to improve performance.
XmlPrint, XmlPrintContent and XmlToString: Serialize XML from AST back to string.
XmlGetAttribute, XmlGetElement, XmlGetElements and XmlGetValue: Helper functions for reading XML in AST.

topic.p:
IMPORTANT: The XML parser only accept a subset of the XML standard:
Prologue nodes only accept instructions and comments.
Elements only accept elements, text nodes, cdata nodes and comments as sub nodes.
<(<), >(>), &(&), '('), "(") must be escaped in attribute values and text nodes.
The xml:space attribute is ignored during parsing.

topic.title: Writing your own parsers.
topic.p: You are recommended to learn EBNF before using the parser generator.

To write your own parser:
Prepare a grammar file for your parser.
Use ParserGen.exe from the release to process this file.
Some C++ source files will be generated in the same folder that contains the grammar file. Add them to your project.
For example, if you have such configuration in your grammar file:
classPrefix:Xml parser:ParseDocument(XDocument)
Then you can call XmlParseDocument("Input", XmlLoadTable()) to do parsing. You are recommended to cache the return value from the load table function.

Here are some sample grammar files:
JSON parser (VlppParser2 version).
XML parser (VlppParser2 version).
Workflow script parser (VlppParser2 version).
Instance query parser (VlppParser2 version) for GacUI XML resource, working like the CSS selector but it is inspired by XPath.
